channel.title: PBS News Hour - The Latest
channel.description: The latest news, analysis and reporting from PBS News Hour.
channel.copyright: NewsHour Productions LLC. All Rights Reserved. 2026

item.title: Trump to travel to Michigan ahead of swing state's primary election
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to rally in Michigan next week as early in-person voting begins in the battleground state's primary, where voters will choose nominees for open Senate and governor's races.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press

item.title: U.S. military launches 13th night strikes on Iran as clashes escalate over shipping routes
item.description: U.S. Central Command said the latest attacks are designed to "further degrade Iran's ability to threaten civilian mariners and commercial vessels transiting regional waters" as the Americans push to regain control over the Strait of Hormuz and restore the flow of international shipping.
item.dc:creator: Elena Becatoros, Associated Press
Fatma Khaled, Associated Press
Collin Binkley, Associated Press

item.title: OpenAI blamed a hacking event on its AI models going rogue. Here's what to know
item.description: ChatGPT maker OpenAI says it is still investigating the "unprecedented cyber incident" that led its artificial intelligence systems to break out of a testing environment and hack into another AI company.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press

item.title: Trump imposes double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries as stopgap 10% levies expire Friday
item.description: President Donald Trump is going ahead with new double-digit tariffs on dozens of U.S. trading partners just as the clock runs out Friday on stopgap levies he imposed after a stinging defeat at the Supreme Court.
item.dc:creator: Paul Wiseman, Associated Press
Mae Anderson, Associated Press

item.title: Earnings at Musk's Tesla fall as spending on research cuts into profit from selling cars
item.description: While the core car business had a solid quarter, Tesla is now focused on building out the infrastructure and AI software that underpins its robotaxis and robotics businesses, which Musk has said are the future of the company. And doing so requires a lot more spending.
item.dc:creator: Alex Veiga, Associated Press

item.title: Trump threatens 'major military punishment' after Houthi attacks on tankers in Red Sea
item.description: International oil prices topped $100 a barrel after Houthi rebels in Yemen claimed they attacked two oil tankers in the Red Sea, raising new fears of disruptions to global energy supplies. In response, President Trump warned that if the Houthis attack another ship, the U.S. would inflict "major military punishment." He also warned of escalation inside Iran. Nick Schifrin reports.
item.dc:creator: Nick Schifrin
Dan Sagalyn

item.title: Former U.S. diplomat breaks down Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping
item.description: For perspective on the Houthis' efforts to block shipping in the Red Sea, Amna Nawaz spoke with Timothy Lenderking. During his decades-long career as a U.S. diplomat, he served as special envoy to Yemen and as acting assistant secretary for Near East Affairs. He's now at the law firm Squire Patton Boggs.
item.dc:creator: Amna Nawaz
Dan Sagalyn

item.title: News Wrap: Trump announces new tariffs on dozens of countries
item.description: In our news wrap Thursday, the Trump administration is imposing new tariffs on dozens of countries starting the exact moment a prior round of tariffs is set to expire, tensions are running high in Madison, Wisconsin, following the fatal police shooting of a man with a knife and the man who killed Minnesota House Speaker Melissa Hortman was sentenced to life in prison.

item.title: Trump reluctant to pressure China after claiming election interference
item.description: In his address about foreign interference in elections, President Trump made sweeping claims about China. But when asked this week whether he would raise the issue directly with Beijing, Trump appeared to downplay his own warning. That's unfolding as Democrats are raising concerns about the president's focus on elections and what he and his allies might do ahead of midterms. Liz Landers reports.
item.dc:creator: Liz Landers
Doug Adams

item.title: FDA considers widening access to peptides promoted by wellness influencers
item.description: An FDA advisory committee began a two-day meeting to consider lifting a ban and widening access to seven popular peptides. They have seen a surge of support from wellness influencers and others, including Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. Amna Nawaz discussed more with Dr. Dhruv Khullar, who wrote the New Yorker article, "Why Are People Injecting Themselves with Peptides?"
item.dc:creator: Andrew Merlis

item.title: End of migrant protections creates elder care staffing challenges
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of migrants are facing uncertainty after the Supreme Court cleared the way for the Trump administration to end Temporary Protected Status for Syrian and Haitian nationals. It's raising alarm among leaders in the senior care industry, who say workers with TPS make up a significant part of a strained workforce. Geoff Bennett discussed more with Katie Smith Sloan of LeadingAge.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Bennett
Jonah Anderson

item.title: Thousands are evacuated as wildfire spreads near popular French tourist town
item.description: About 20,000 people were evacuated from towns and vacation resorts along the French Atlantic coast Thursday as the latest in a series of wildfires raged and hundreds of firefighters were dispatched to tackle it.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press

item.title: Belarusian opposition leader on fighting for democracy under Lukashenko
item.description: Belarus has been plunged into the center of Russia's conflict with Ukraine, both by its borders and by Putin-linked President Alexander Lukashenko. As the eastern European nation of 9 million grapples with its dictatorial leader and the fallout from the war, Liz Landers spoke to opposition leader Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya about the state of the nation and Belarusians' hope for democracy.
item.dc:creator: Liz Landers

item.title: New exhibit showcases the long-hidden artwork of Avital Sagalyn
item.description: At the tip of Cape Cod, a new exhibition is showcasing the work of an artist who spent two summers there in the 1940s. For much of her life, she remained largely unknown in the art world — by her own design. But now, as her paintings receive greater attention, that is beginning to change. Jared Bowen of GBH Boston introduces us to the work of Avital Sagalyn for our arts and culture series, CANVAS.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bowen, GBH

item.title: Ben Wikler and Geoff Bennett discuss the Democrats' plan on 'Settle In'
item.description: There's no shortage of analysis about what's wrong with the Democratic Party. Ben Wikler is focused on what Democrats plan to do about it. The former Wisconsin Democratic Party chair wrote "This Is the Plan," laying out lessons from one of the most closely divided states and what they could mean for Democrats nationwide. Geoff Bennett spoke with him for our PBS News podcast, "Settle In."
item.dc:creator: Sam Lane

item.title: Trump administration admits legal errors and withdraws subpoenas of 3 NYT reporters
item.description: Criticized by a federal judge for sloppy legal work, the government on Thursday grudgingly withdrew subpoenas that would have compelled three New York Times reporters to testify about their sources for articles about President Donald Trump's Qatari-gifted Air Force One jet.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press
Eric Tucker, Associated Press
Larry Neumeister, Associated Press

item.title: Police officer's fatal shooting of man with knife roils Madison, Wisconsin
item.description: Protesters burst into a Thursday news conference about the police shooting of a homeless man in Madison, taking the microphone from the police chief and arguing with some attendees who said they wanted to hear more about the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press

item.title: U.S. filings for unemployment aid fall to 187,000 last week, fewest since 1969
item.description: U.S. applications for jobless benefits tumbled to the lowest level in more than five decades last week as layoffs remain historically low despite global economic uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Matt Ott, Associated Press

item.title: WATCH LIVE: Trump hosts World Series champion LA Dodgers at the White House
item.description: President Donald Trump is welcoming Shohei Ohtani and the reigning World Series champion Los Angeles Dodgers to the White House on Thursday with a Rose Garden event celebrating their second title in as many years.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press
Darlene Superville, Associated Press

item.title: Man who killed Minnesota lawmaker sentenced to life as her family describes trauma, loss
item.description: Friends and family of Minnesota Democratic lawmaker Melissa Hortman said the man sentenced Thursday to two life terms plus 40 years after pleading guilty to her murder failed to eliminate her legacy but caused enduring fear and fury as he changed their lives forever.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Schmidt, Associated Press
Claudia Lauer, Associated Press
